meta.homeTitle: Wavio. Your music, your player, your way.
meta.homeDescription: A open-source Android music player for Navidrome, Jellyfin and OpenSubsonic servers or a local library. No ads, no tracking, fully offline-ready.
meta.privacyDescription: Wavio runs entirely on your device and talks only to the music server you configure. We don't collect, sell, or share your data.

hero.lead: A Android music player for Navidrome, Jellyfin and OpenSubsonic servers or a local library. Built for people who own their library and want it to feel like home.

features.sub: Every screen is built for the way you actually listen. Fast, quiet, and entirely on your terms.

home.label: Home
home.title: Your library, beautifully sorted.
home.desc: Recently played, recently added, most played and internet radio, all on one calm home screen.

audiophile.label: Audiophile
audiophile.title: Tuned, not loud.
audiophile.desc: Audio quality settings, endless playback, replay gain, system EQ.

quote.label: No strings
quote.title: No ads. No accounts. No tracking. The way music apps used to be.

queue.label: Queue
queue.title: A queue you actually control.
queue.desc: Reorder, edit, clear. Save the current queue as a playlist with one tap.

servers.label: Servers
servers.title: Switch backends in one tap.
servers.desc: Run Navidrome at home, Jellyfin at a friend's, OpenSubsonic somewhere else and even a local library for backup. One app for all of them.

sleep.label: Sleep timer
sleep.title: Fade gracefully.
sleep.desc: Schedule a soft fade-out so your favorite album can carry you under.

podcasts.label: Podcasts
podcasts.title: Learn something everywhere you go.
podcasts.desc: Search, follow and play podcasts powered by Taddy. Your tracks and your shows, under one roof.

radio.label: Internet radio
radio.title: Tune in, anywhere.
radio.desc: Stream the internet radio stations from your server in a tap. Browse, save, and listen live.

lyrics.label: Lyrics
lyrics.title: Sing along, in sync.
lyrics.desc: Synchronised lyrics from LRCLib, scrolling line by line with the track.

widgets.label: Home screen widgets
widgets.title: A glance away.
widgets.desc: Now playing and recently played, right on your Android home screen. No need to open the app.

showcase.sub: Views built around what you do most. Browse, play, queue, tune, download, and forget the app is even there.

final.desc: Free, open-source, and built by people who think your library should belong to you.

footer.tagline: A open-source music player for Android. Compatible with Navidrome, Jellyfin and OpenSubsonic or a local library.
footer.madeWith: Made with Astro and TailwindCSS. Hosted on Vercel. All texts use the Inter font.

privacy.summary: Wavio runs entirely on your device and talks only to the music server you configure. We don't collect, sell, or share your data, because we don't have it.

sections.title: Who we are
sections.body: Wavio is a free, open-source Android application maintained by independent contributors. The source code is published under the MIT license and available for anyone to inspect on GitHub.

sections.title: What Wavio collects about you
sections.body:
Nothing personal. Wavio has no accounts, no profiles, no advertising SDKs, no behavioural analytics, and no social trackers. We do not operate any server that stores who you are or what you listen to.

The only data leaving your phone is what's needed to play music or to help us diagnose crashes, described in the next two sections.

sections.title: What stays on your device
sections.body:
To work as a music player, Wavio stores the following locally, on your phone only:

  • Server connection details (URL, username, and an encrypted password or token) for each server you add.
  • Your playback queue, recently played and recently searched items, sleep timer and equalizer settings, and other app preferences.
  • Tracks you choose to download for offline listening, plus cached album art and metadata fetched from your server.
  • Podcast API credentials (Taddy) if you enable podcasts.

This information lives in app-private storage. Uninstalling Wavio removes all of it.

sections.title: What your music server sees
sections.body:
When you connect Wavio to a Navidrome, Jellyfin, or OpenSubsonic instance, the app communicates directly with that server using the credentials you provide. The server will typically log your IP address, the requests you make, and your listening activity, just like any other client would.

Wavio's contributors do not operate those servers and have no access to what they log.

Please consult the privacy policy of the server you connect to.

sections.title: Crash and error reporting
sections.body:
Production builds of Wavio use Sentry to report crashes and unexpected errors so we can fix them. Each report includes technical context such as a stack trace, the app version, the device model, the Android version, and the IP address that delivered the report.

We do not assign you an identifier, do not track which screens you visit, do not log what you listen to, and do not build a profile of you. Reports are used only to diagnose bugs and are retained according to Sentry's data retention policy.

sections.title: Android permissions
sections.body:
Wavio requests only the permissions it needs to play music:

  • Internet to reach your music server and the third-party services listed below.
  • Foreground service and media playback to keep audio playing when the screen is off.
  • Notifications to display playback controls on the lock screen and in the notification shade.
  • Modify audio settings to apply the system equalizer.
  • Read media audio and storage to save and read downloaded tracks for offline playback.
  • Vibration for subtle haptic feedback on certain controls.

Wavio does not request access to your contacts, your precise location, the camera, or any other sensitive data.

sections.title: Third-party services
sections.body:
Beyond your music server and Sentry, Wavio talks to a small number of external services, and only when the corresponding feature is used:

  • LRCLib is queried for synchronised lyrics when you open the lyrics view for a track.
  • Taddy powers podcast search and metadata, and is only contacted if you provide your own Taddy API key and user ID in the settings.
  • Radio Browser provides the internet radio station directory, and is queried when you browse or search for radio stations.
  • Google Cast is used when you cast playback to a Chromecast-compatible device. Google's terms then apply to that session.

No advertising network, no analytics provider, and no social tracker is integrated into Wavio.

sections.title: Children
sections.body: Wavio is suitable for users of all ages and does not knowingly collect any information about anyone, including children.

sections.title: Changes to this policy
sections.body: If this policy ever changes, the updated version will be published here and announced in the project's release notes on GitHub.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: Questions, concerns, or curiosity? Open an issue on the Wavio GitHub repository and a maintainer will get back to you.
